question: Is psychological science reproducible?
title: Comment on "Estimating the reproducibility of psychological science".
text: A paper from the Open Science Collaboration (Research Articles, 28 August 2015, aac4716) attempting to replicate 100 published studies suggests that the reproducibility of psychological science is surprisingly low. We show that this article contains three statistical errors and provides no support for such a conclusion. Indeed, the data are consistent with the opposite conclusion, namely, that the reproducibility of psychological science is quite high.

finding.value: Reproducibility of psychological science is actually high, not low
finding.quote: the data are consistent with the opposite conclusion, namely, that the reproducibility of psychological science is quite high

model.value: Re-analysis/critique of Open Science Collaboration replication dataset
model.quote: A paper from the Open Science Collaboration ... attempting to replicate 100 published studies

intervention.quote: We show that this article contains three statistical errors

assay.value: Statistical/methodological error analysis of replication study
assay.quote: this article contains three statistical errors

endpoint.value: Reproducibility of psychological science (validity of OSC's conclusion)
endpoint.quote: the reproducibility of psychological science is surprisingly low

statistic.value: Three statistical errors identified invalidating low-reproducibility conclusion
statistic.quote: this article contains three statistical errors and provides no support for such a conclusion
